subject: Adding DefaultServlet to support static resources
commit_message:
Adding DefaultServlet to support static resources

Adding DefaultServlet to make embeded jetty not only
support Jersey, but also support static resources, like
html, css and js.

Issue-ID: POLICY-2311
Signed-off-by: Hengye <yehui.wang@est.tech>
Change-Id: I71309036627d75dcc56947b395688a4e2f22c0ce

Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 1:

(4 comments)

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> (2 comments)
> 
> it feels like this should be done via subclassing instead of by adding methods to the existing class & interface

I also considerred it before, but finally I decide to add APIs to HttpServletServer and JettyJerseyServer.

Because DefaultServlet is one type of servlets(just like jersey sertlet), it is used to handle static resources for jetty server. Both jersey and default servlet are managed by a single ServletContextHandler provided by one jetty server. 
From this point of view, adding static resource handling to HttpServletServer and JettyJerseyServer as a supplementary function is meanningful.

still feels like this should be done via separate classes (using subclassing, and possibly a common superclass - though not necessary right now), where one class supports services based on classpath and the other supports services based on resources.  Adding a service to one uses the normal stuff, while adding a service to the other uses DefaultServlet.class

Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

I'll defer to Jorge on this.  I'm not really comfortable adding this as additional methods on HttpClient, but maybe it's OK, provided we fix it next release.

Patch Set 4:

> I'll defer to Jorge on this.  I'm not really comfortable adding
 > this as additional methods on HttpClient, but maybe it's OK,
 > provided we fix it next release.

I think Jim's proposal to move the functionality out of JettyJerseyServer is best under the following assumption:

"We won't have cases (either no technically possible, or design wise not desirable) where a (jaxrs) Jersey Servlet and a (resource) DefaultServlet will coexist under the same umbrella HTTP Server.   So in other words are mutually exclusive, and from that it is a better approach to separate it into different entities, classes.".  

Would this be a fair way to put it Jim?
